items.title: Massive $920 discount knocks Samsung’s 49-inch Odyssey OLED G9 240Hz curved gaming monitor down to its lowest price
items.summary:
As part of its wider sale on Samsung monitors, Woot is now offering the brand’s 49-inch Odyssey OLED G9 (G95SD) curved model for $979.99 Prime shipped (a $6 delivery fee will apply otherwise). This OLED display hit the scene carrying a $1,900 price tag, and today’s offer knocks $920 off that price to mark a new all-time low for the model. For comparison, the same monitor is still listed at $1,900 directly from Samsung, while Amazon currently has it at $1,198, making Woot’s deal a clear winner here if you’re looking to upgrade. It’s a pretty good price for one of Samsung’s popular ultrawide designs, and you can learn more about it in detail below.

items.title: Best Buy Tech Fest sale delivers up to $750 off Copilot+ and gaming laptops, deals from $170 + FREE $50 credits
items.summary:
Best Buy has kicked off its new Tech Fest sale, bringing a fresh wave of discounts across PCs, Apple gear, home appliances, and more. In addition to the deals we recently saw alongside its Apple sale, this event introduces new price drops across a wider range of Windows laptops and desktops, with savings of up to $700 on machines starting at just $170. The sale also features a variety of Copilot+ PCs and gaming laptops, all in brand-new condition. Notably, Best Buy Plus and Total members can also score a $50 bonus reward credit when making a PC or a Mac purchase of $499 or more, adding even more value to the ongoing deals. We’ve rounded up some of the standout offers from the Tech Fest sale below, so be sure to check them out.

The 512GB model is also discounted to $93.70 shipped, down from its $120 price. Prices on SSDs, memory cards, and other storage devices have been steadily climbing lately, so it might be worth jumping on this deal while it’s live. items.title: Today is your last chance at the 2026 Mario Day Switch game deals
items.summary:
Mario Day 2026 was an eventful one this year and today is your last shot at the official Switch game deals. While many of the physical game deals are long gone, there are still loads of notable digital titles live at Amazon, Best Buy, the eShop, and elsewhere through today only. From the final Super Mario Galaxy Movie trailer and the release of Mario Clash, Mario vs. Donkey Kong, and Mario Tennis classics, to the reveal of upcoming Super Mario minifigure scale LEGO sets and the Mario Kart Luigi build, we also got an official release date for Yoshi and the Mysterious Book and a giant lineup of Switch game deals too. Today, however, is your last chance at the a latter as the last of the Mario Switch game deals are scheduled to be gone for another year at midnight PT tonight. Check them out below.
